world.title: Minnesota Wild reporter Pierce and her three children found dead in house fire, league says
world.description: NHL reporter Jessi Pierce and her three children were killed Saturday in a weekend house fire in Minnesota, the league announced on its sports website Sunday.

world.title: Iran threatens to close Strait of Hormuz after Trump's 48-hour ultimatum
world.description: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz would be closed immediately if the US follows up on US President Donald Trump's threat to attack its power plants.

world.title: LETTER: Re: Militarism is the downfall of the Canadian state
world.description: The downside is that the Canadian government is axing thousands of jobs (40,000 civil service posts) to bolster our military spending

world.title: Far from the frontline, close to the fallout: How the Middle East war is testing Australia
world.description: Australia is far from the Middle East conflict — but not untouched by it. As risks ripple outwards, Canberra's role is quietly being tested.

world.title: Keir Starmer and Donald Trump discuss need to reopen Hormuz Strait
world.description: No 10 says the leaders agree reopening the shipping lane is key to ensuring energy market stability.

world.title: A War Seen From Above
world.description: In the fields of Zaporizhzhia, Ukrainian reconnaissance crews track movement, guide strikes, and adapt to a battlefield where drones reshape distance, timing, and survival.

world.title: Slovenia's liberals and populists neck and neck, preliminary results show
world.description: Early results of Slovenia’s highly contested parliamentary election show governing liberals and opposition right-wing populists neck and neck, heralding a period of political uncertainty in the small European Union country.

world.title: PM Modi is India's longest serving head of govt
world.description: India News: NEW DELHI: PM Narendra Modi on Sunday reached the milestone of being the longest serving head of govt, which includes his tenure as Gujarat CM, drawin.

business.title: Musk says Tesla, SpaceX, xAI chip project to kick off in Texas
business.description: Musk said his Terafab project — a grand plan to eventually manufacture his own chips for robotics, artificial intelligence and space data centers — will be built in Austin.

business.title: Service stations audited by National Measurement Institute
business.description: Petrol stations that rip off customers face fines of up to $220,000 during a national fuel audit this week.

business.description: The system could potentially figure out the fastest way to get the cars charged, by prioritizing the cars with the highest voltages.

business.title: Oil prices rise after Trump issues ultimatum and Iran threatens to close the Strait of Hormuz indefinitely
business.description: Oil prices rose Sunday after President Donald Trump threatened to “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Monday evening.

business.title: Air India restores last of grounded fleet to operations
business.description: With VT‑ALL’s return Air India has successfully revived every single one of the 30 previously grounded aircraft.

science.title: Scientists discover a plant that could survive on Mars and help create a ‘Green Planet’
science.description: Science News: Scientists have made an astonishing finding that brings us closer than ever to the concept of ‘Green Mars’, by discovering a desert moss species - Syn.

science.title: Humans, animals like same mating calls, backing `Darwin's hunch'
science.description: New research from The Music Lab points towards humans and other animals having common tastes in sound.

science.title: Bird feathers can cool off by sending body heat into space
science.description: New study shows that bird feathers do more than look pretty. They help birds manage heat and stay cool in surprising ways.

science.title: $17 million mission to find life above Venus
science.description: Rocket Lab boss Sir Peter Beck says the price tag for his mission to find life in the clouds above Venus later this year is cheap by space exploration...

science.title: Fiber-optic sensors reveal how farming destroys soil's natural structure
science.description: Soil is often perceived simply as "dirt," but in reality, it is a dynamic, living system that acts as Earth's natural sponge. Unfortunately, common agricultural practices—including deep plowing and the use of heavy machinery—can severely disrupt this natural system, according to a new study led by Dr. Shi Qibin from the Institute of Geology and Geophysics of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, in collaboration with international partners.

science.title: Study maps particulate thiols across western North Pacific, tracing them to phytoplankton
science.description: In Science of The Total Environment, researchers demonstrate the broad distribution of particulate thiols in the western North Pacific and show that their main source is marine phytoplankton. The analysis indicates that differences in thiol concentrations between ocean areas are significantly influenced by water mass properties, phytoplankton composition, and environmental stress.

science.title: Tooth enamel emerges as key archive of ancient biomolecules
science.description: Amino acids have been discovered in 48-million-year-old fossil teeth, revealing new insights into ancient life and evolution

ai.title: K‑Tech (NASDAQ: KMRK) enters JV to develop up to 500MW AI/HPC infrastructure in Canada
ai.description: Hong Kong-based K‑Tech and Calgary’s Aurora AZ Energy form joint venture to power large‑scale AI, HPC and crypto mining operations in Alberta with low‑cost wellhead natural gas.

ai.title: SpaceX and Tesla will build advanced chip factories in Austin
ai.description: SpaceX and Tesla will build two advanced chip factories at a sprawling facility in Austin, Texas, one to power cars and humanoid robots, and another designed for artificial intelligence data centers in space, CEO Elon Musk said on Sunday.

ai.title: Musk's 'Terafab': Pioneering AI Chip Factories to Propel Space and Robotics
ai.description: Elon Musk announced plans to build 'Terafab,' two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas. One will power Tesla vehicles and humanoid robots; the other will be for AI data centers in space. Musk emphasized the urgent need for these facilities to meet future demands, despite existing global chip suppliers.

ai.title: Why everyone hates NVIDIA DLSS 5 (but will love it eventually)
ai.description: DLSS 5: from “next-gen magic” to “makeup filter gone rogue”. Hated today, inevitable tomorrow, as AI learns that vibes matter as much as pixels.
